content.question: What does the atomic number of an element represent?
options: The number of neutrons in the nucleus
The number of protons in the nucleus
The total mass of the atom
content.correct_answer: The number of protons in the nucleus
content.explanation: The atomic number is the number of protons in an atom's nucleus. It uniquely identifies each element — carbon always has 6 protons, oxygen always has 8. The atomic number also equals the number of electrons in a neutral atom.

content.question: Elements in the same group (column) of the periodic table share which property?
options: The same number of outer (valence) electrons
The same state of matter at room temperature
content.correct_answer: The same number of outer (valence) electrons
content.explanation: Elements in the same group have the same number of valence (outer shell) electrons. This gives them similar chemical properties. All Group 1 elements (alkali metals) have 1 valence electron and react vigorously with water.

content.question: Why are noble gases (Group 18) generally unreactive?
options: They are too heavy to react
They have a full outer electron shell and are chemically stable
content.correct_answer: They have a full outer electron shell and are chemically stable
content.explanation: Noble gases (helium, neon, argon, etc.) have 8 valence electrons — a full outer shell. Atoms react to gain or lose electrons to achieve a full shell. Noble gases are already stable, so they rarely form compounds.

content.question: Which of the following is NOT evidence that a chemical reaction has occurred?
options: A precipitate (solid) forms in a liquid
content.correct_answer: Ice melting in warm water
content.explanation: Ice melting is a physical change — water changes state but no new substance is formed. Chemical reaction signs include: gas production, color change, precipitate forming, energy release (heat/light), and irreversibility.

content.question: In the equation: 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O, what are the reactants?
content.correct_answer: H₂ and O₂
content.explanation: Reactants are the starting materials (on the left of the arrow). Products are what's formed (on the right). Here hydrogen gas (H₂) and oxygen gas (O₂) react to form water (H₂O).

content.question: If 10 g of reactants combine in a closed container and the reaction produces a gas, how much do all the products together weigh?
options: Less than 10 g because gas escapes
More than 10 g because energy is added
content.correct_answer: Exactly 10 g
content.explanation: The Law of Conservation of Mass states that matter is neither created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction. The total mass of products always equals the total mass of reactants. Atoms rearrange — they don't disappear.

content.question: On the pH scale, what does a pH of 7 indicate?
content.correct_answer: A neutral substance
content.explanation: The pH scale runs from 0–14. pH 7 is neutral (pure water). Below 7 is acidic (the lower, the stronger). Above 7 is basic/alkaline (the higher, the stronger). Stomach acid is about pH 2; bleach is about pH 12.

content.question: A ball is rolling across a frictionless surface. According to Newton's First Law, what will happen?
options: It will gradually slow down and stop
It will speed up over time
It will continue moving at the same speed in the same direction forever
content.correct_answer: It will continue moving at the same speed in the same direction forever
content.explanation: Newton's First Law (Law of Inertia): an object in motion stays in motion, and an object at rest stays at rest, unless acted on by an external force. With no friction, nothing stops the ball.

content.question: A 5 kg object is pushed with a net force of 20 N. What is its acceleration?
content.correct_answer: 4 m/s²
content.explanation: Newton's Second Law: F = ma, so a = F ÷ m. a = 20 N ÷ 5 kg = 4 m/s². The greater the force, the greater the acceleration. The greater the mass, the less acceleration for the same force.

content.question: A rocket launches by expelling hot gas downward. According to Newton's Third Law, what happens?
options: The gas pushes the rocket downward too
The expelled gas pushes the rocket upward with equal force
The rocket slows down as gas is lost
Nothing acts on the rocket — only gravity matters
content.correct_answer: The expelled gas pushes the rocket upward with equal force
content.explanation: Newton's Third Law: for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. The rocket pushes gas down; the gas pushes the rocket up with the same force. This is how all rockets and jet engines work.

content.question: What is the key difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
options: Eukaryotes have a membrane-bound nucleus; prokaryotes do not
Prokaryotes have mitochondria; eukaryotes do not
content.correct_answer: Eukaryotes have a membrane-bound nucleus; prokaryotes do not
content.explanation: Eukaryotic cells (plants, animals, fungi) have a nucleus enclosed in a membrane, along with other organelles. Prokaryotic cells (bacteria) have no membrane-bound nucleus — their DNA floats in the cytoplasm.

content.question: Why is the mitochondrion called the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
options: It controls what enters and exits the cell
It produces ATP (energy) through cellular respiration
It builds proteins from amino acids
It stores the cell's genetic information
content.correct_answer: It produces ATP (energy) through cellular respiration
content.explanation: Mitochondria convert glucose and oxygen into ATP (adenosine triphosphate) — the energy currency cells use for all their functions. The process is called cellular respiration: C₆H₁₂O₆ + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O + ATP.

content.question: The cell membrane is described as 'selectively permeable.' What does this mean?
options: It is completely impermeable — nothing passes through
It allows all substances to pass through freely
It allows some substances to pass while blocking others
It only allows water to pass through
content.correct_answer: It allows some substances to pass while blocking others
content.explanation: Selectively permeable means the membrane controls what enters and exits the cell. Small molecules like water and oxygen pass freely. Large or charged molecules need special protein channels or active transport.

content.question: Which is the correct equation for photosynthesis?
content.correct_answer: CO₂ + H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + O₂
content.explanation: Photosynthesis: carbon dioxide + water + light energy → glucose + oxygen. Plants absorb CO₂ and H₂O, use light energy to build glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆), and release O₂ as a byproduct. The reverse is cellular respiration.

content.question: What is the shape of a DNA molecule?
content.correct_answer: Double helix
content.explanation: DNA is shaped like a twisted ladder — a double helix. The 'rungs' are pairs of nitrogenous bases (A-T and G-C). This structure was discovered by Watson and Crick in 1953, using X-ray data from Rosalind Franklin.

content.question: In DNA, adenine (A) always pairs with _______, and guanine (G) always pairs with _______.
content.correct_answer: Thymine (T) and Cytosine (C)
content.explanation: DNA base pairing rules: A pairs with T (adenine-thymine), and G pairs with C (guanine-cytosine). These are called complementary base pairs. If one strand reads ATGC, the other reads TACG.

content.question: A pea plant has one dominant allele for tallness (T) and one recessive allele for shortness (t). What will the plant look like?
options: Short, because the recessive allele overpowers it
Tall, because the dominant allele masks the recessive one
content.correct_answer: Tall, because the dominant allele masks the recessive one
content.explanation: When one dominant allele (T) and one recessive allele (t) are present, the dominant trait is expressed. The plant is 'Tt' — heterozygous. Only 'tt' (two recessive alleles) produces a short plant.

content.question: Two heterozygous tall pea plants (Tt × Tt) are crossed. What percentage of offspring will be short (tt)?
content.explanation: Using a Punnett square: Tt × Tt gives TT, Tt, Tt, tt. That's 1 TT : 2 Tt : 1 tt. Only tt is short = 1 out of 4 = 25%. This is Mendel's 3:1 ratio.

content.question: What is a genetic mutation?
options: A change in an organism's behavior
A permanent change in the DNA sequence
The transfer of traits from parent to offspring
content.correct_answer: A permanent change in the DNA sequence
content.explanation: A mutation is a change in the nucleotide sequence of DNA. Mutations can be caused by copying errors, radiation, or chemicals. Some are harmful, some neutral, and rarely, some are beneficial — driving evolution.

content.question: Which biome receives less than 25 cm of rain per year and experiences extreme temperature swings between day and night?
content.correct_answer: Desert
content.explanation: Deserts receive very little precipitation (under 25 cm/year) and can have dramatic temperature swings — scorching hot by day when sand absorbs heat, and cold at night when that heat radiates away quickly.

content.question: Clownfish live among sea anemone tentacles without being stung. The anemone eats scraps dropped by the clownfish. This relationship is an example of:
content.correct_answer: Mutualism
content.explanation: Mutualism is a symbiotic relationship where both species benefit. The clownfish gets protection from predators; the anemone gets food scraps and cleaning. Both win. Parasitism harms the host; commensalism benefits only one.

content.question: How does burning fossil fuels affect the carbon cycle?
options: It removes carbon from the atmosphere
It releases stored carbon back into the atmosphere as CO₂
content.correct_answer: It releases stored carbon back into the atmosphere as CO₂
content.explanation: Fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas) are ancient organic matter — carbon stored underground for millions of years. Burning them releases that carbon as CO₂. This adds carbon to the atmosphere faster than natural cycles can absorb it, contributing to climate change.

content.question: Only about 10% of energy passes from one trophic level to the next. If producers contain 10,000 kcal of energy, how much energy is available to secondary consumers?
content.correct_answer: 100 kcal
content.explanation: The 10% rule: 10,000 kcal (producers) → 1,000 kcal (primary consumers) → 100 kcal (secondary consumers). 90% is lost as heat at each level. This is why food chains rarely exceed 4–5 levels.

content.question: What is the relationship between the frequency and wavelength of a wave if the speed stays constant?
content.correct_answer: Higher frequency = shorter wavelength
content.explanation: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength. If speed is constant, they're inversely proportional: increase frequency → decrease wavelength, and vice versa. Think of sound: higher pitch (frequency) = shorter waves.

content.question: Which type of electromagnetic radiation has the highest energy?
content.correct_answer: Gamma rays
content.explanation: Energy in the EM spectrum increases with frequency. Order from lowest to highest energy: radio → microwave → infrared → visible → UV → X-ray → gamma rays. Gamma rays have the shortest wavelength and highest frequency.

content.question: A metal spoon placed in hot soup becomes hot quickly. This is an example of:
content.correct_answer: Conduction
content.explanation: Conduction is heat transfer through direct contact between particles. Metals are excellent conductors because their free electrons transfer energy quickly. Convection moves heat through fluid circulation; radiation transfers heat through electromagnetic waves.

content.question: Carbon-12 and Carbon-14 are isotopes of carbon. What makes them different?
options: They have different numbers of protons
They have different numbers of electrons
They have different numbers of neutrons
content.correct_answer: They have different numbers of neutrons
content.explanation: Isotopes are atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons. Carbon-12 has 6 protons and 6 neutrons; Carbon-14 has 6 protons and 8 neutrons. Same element, different mass. Carbon-14 is radioactive and used in dating ancient materials.

content.question: A population of beetles lives on dark tree bark. Most are brown, but a few are green. Birds easily spot and eat the green beetles. Over many generations, what happens?
options: Green beetles evolve to become darker on purpose
Brown beetles become the dominant color through natural selection
content.correct_answer: Brown beetles become the dominant color through natural selection
content.explanation: Natural selection: organisms with traits better suited to their environment survive and reproduce more. Brown beetles survive longer, pass on their genes. Over generations, brown becomes more common. Evolution isn't intentional — it's differential survival.

content.question: What is the purpose of mitosis?
options: To produce sex cells (eggs and sperm)
To produce two genetically identical daughter cells for growth and repair
To reduce the chromosome number by half
To combine DNA from two parents
content.correct_answer: To produce two genetically identical daughter cells for growth and repair
content.explanation: Mitosis produces two identical copies of the original cell, used for growth, tissue repair, and asexual reproduction. Meiosis is different — it produces 4 sex cells with half the chromosomes, enabling sexual reproduction.

content.question: Why is it important that meiosis produces cells with half the normal chromosome number (haploid)?
options: So cells are smaller and fit in reproductive organs
So when two sex cells combine at fertilization, the offspring has the correct total chromosome number
So offspring are genetically identical to one parent
content.correct_answer: So when two sex cells combine at fertilization, the offspring has the correct total chromosome number
content.explanation: Humans have 46 chromosomes (23 pairs). Meiosis halves this to 23. When sperm (23) and egg (23) unite at fertilization, the resulting zygote has 46 — the correct full number. Without this halving, chromosome numbers would double every generation.

content.question: Which of the following is a chemical change (not just physical)?
content.correct_answer: Rusting iron
content.explanation: Rusting is a chemical change — iron reacts with oxygen and water to form iron oxide, a new substance with different properties. Physical changes (cutting, dissolving sugar, crushing) alter form but not chemical composition.

content.question: You push a wall with all your strength but it doesn't move. In the scientific sense, how much work have you done on the wall?
options: A large amount — you exerted a lot of force
Some — depending on how hard you pushed
Zero — because the wall didn't move
content.correct_answer: Zero — because the wall didn't move
content.explanation: In physics, Work = Force × Distance. If the wall doesn't move (distance = 0), then work = 0, regardless of how much force was applied. You felt tired, but scientifically, you did no work on the wall.

content.question: A roller coaster at the top of its highest hill has maximum _______; at the bottom of the hill moving fastest, it has maximum _______.
content.correct_answer: potential energy; kinetic energy
content.explanation: At the top: height is maximum, so gravitational potential energy (PE = mgh) is maximum, speed is minimum. At the bottom: height is zero, PE converts to kinetic energy (KE = ½mv²), so speed is maximum.

content.question: Which property do metals generally have that nonmetals do not?
options: They are poor conductors of electricity
They are gases at room temperature
They conduct heat and electricity well
content.correct_answer: They conduct heat and electricity well
content.explanation: Metals are good conductors because their outer electrons are loosely held and can move freely. Nonmetals generally don't conduct electricity (except graphite). Metals are also usually shiny, malleable, and have high melting points.

content.question: Color blindness is a recessive, X-linked trait. Why is it more common in males than females?
options: Males have stronger reactions to recessive genes
Males only have one X chromosome, so one recessive allele is enough to express the trait
The Y chromosome promotes color blindness
Females are immune to X-linked conditions
content.correct_answer: Males only have one X chromosome, so one recessive allele is enough to express the trait
content.explanation: Males are XY — they have only one X chromosome. If that X carries the color blindness allele, they express the trait. Females are XX — they need two copies of the recessive allele. One normal X is enough to prevent it in females.

content.question: A circuit has a voltage of 12V and a resistance of 4 ohms. What is the current?
content.correct_answer: 3 A
content.explanation: Ohm's Law: V = IR, so I = V ÷ R = 12 ÷ 4 = 3 A. V = voltage (volts), I = current (amps), R = resistance (ohms). Higher resistance reduces current for the same voltage.

content.question: When two tectonic plates collide and one is forced beneath the other, this is called:
content.correct_answer: Subduction
content.explanation: Subduction occurs at convergent boundaries when a denser oceanic plate dives beneath a continental plate. The descending plate melts in the mantle, which can create volcanoes above and deep ocean trenches below.

content.question: Limestone (sedimentary rock) deep underground is subjected to intense heat and pressure. It transforms into marble. This produces which type of rock?
content.correct_answer: Metamorphic
content.explanation: Metamorphic rock forms when existing rocks are transformed by heat and pressure without melting. 'Metamorphic' comes from Greek meaning 'change form.' Limestone → marble and shale → slate are classic examples.

content.question: A red blood cell is placed in a solution with a higher salt concentration than the cell's interior. What happens?
options: Water moves into the cell and it swells
Water moves out of the cell and it shrinks
Salt enters the cell and it swells
Nothing changes because the membrane blocks everything
content.correct_answer: Water moves out of the cell and it shrinks
content.explanation: Osmosis is water moving across a semipermeable membrane from low solute concentration to high. The solution outside has more solute (salt), so water moves out of the cell toward higher concentration, causing the cell to shrink (crenation).

content.question: A car drives 100 km north, then 100 km south, returning to its starting point. After 2 hours total, what is its average velocity?
content.correct_answer: 0 km/h
content.explanation: Velocity is displacement (change in position) ÷ time. The car returned to its start — net displacement is 0. So average velocity = 0 ÷ 2 = 0 km/h. Speed (total distance ÷ time) would be 100 km/h. Velocity includes direction and net change.

content.question: What is the main difference between an ionic bond and a covalent bond?
options: Ionic bonds share electrons; covalent bonds transfer them
Ionic bonds transfer electrons between atoms; covalent bonds share electrons
Ionic bonds only form in gases; covalent bonds only in solids
content.correct_answer: Ionic bonds transfer electrons between atoms; covalent bonds share electrons
content.explanation: Ionic bonds: one atom loses electrons, one gains them, creating oppositely charged ions that attract (e.g., NaCl — table salt). Covalent bonds: atoms share electrons (e.g., H₂O, CO₂). Ionic bonds form between metals and nonmetals; covalent between nonmetals.

content.question: An invasive species is introduced to a new ecosystem. Why can it often outcompete native species?
options: It always reproduces faster than native species
It often has no natural predators in the new environment
It instantly adapts to any new environment
Native species always accept newcomers peacefully
content.correct_answer: It often has no natural predators in the new environment
content.explanation: In its native habitat, an organism is kept in check by predators, parasites, and disease. In a new ecosystem, these controls may not exist, allowing the species to reproduce unchecked and overwhelm native competitors.

content.question: What is the difference between the central nervous system (CNS) and the peripheral nervous system (PNS)?
options: The CNS includes all nerves; the PNS is only in the brain
The CNS is the brain and spinal cord; the PNS is all other nerves in the body
The PNS controls voluntary movement; the CNS controls involuntary
They are two names for the same system
content.correct_answer: The CNS is the brain and spinal cord; the PNS is all other nerves in the body
content.explanation: The CNS (brain + spinal cord) is the command center — it processes information and coordinates responses. The PNS is the network of nerves that carries signals between the CNS and the rest of the body.

content.question: When blood sugar rises after eating, the pancreas releases insulin. What does insulin do?
options: It breaks down fat for energy
It signals cells to absorb glucose, lowering blood sugar
content.correct_answer: It signals cells to absorb glucose, lowering blood sugar
content.explanation: Insulin is a hormone that acts like a key — it 'unlocks' cells so they can absorb glucose from the bloodstream. This lowers blood sugar back to normal. In Type 1 diabetes, the pancreas doesn't produce insulin; in Type 2, cells don't respond to it properly.

content.question: In a drug trial, one group takes the new medication and another takes a sugar pill (placebo). What is the purpose of the placebo group?
options: To serve as a baseline comparison so results can be attributed to the drug
To ensure the drug is tested on unhealthy people
To measure how effective placebos are
content.correct_answer: To serve as a baseline comparison so results can be attributed to the drug
content.explanation: The control group (placebo) shows what happens without the treatment. Any difference between the groups can then be attributed to the drug itself. Without a control, you can't know if improvements were due to the drug or other factors.

content.question: A 2 kg ball moving at 6 m/s collides with a stationary 4 kg ball. If they stick together, what is their combined velocity? (Assume no external forces.)
content.correct_answer: 2 m/s
content.explanation: Conservation of momentum: p before = p after. Before: (2 kg × 6 m/s) + (4 kg × 0) = 12 kg·m/s. After: (2+4) kg × v = 12. v = 12 ÷ 6 = 2 m/s. Momentum is always conserved in a closed system.

content.question: The greenhouse effect is a natural process. Why is it currently a problem?
options: The greenhouse effect has never been natural — it is entirely human-caused
Human activities are increasing greenhouse gas concentrations, trapping more heat than natural levels
The Sun is emitting more radiation than in the past
The ozone layer is growing thicker, trapping more light
content.correct_answer: Human activities are increasing greenhouse gas concentrations, trapping more heat than natural levels
content.explanation: The natural greenhouse effect keeps Earth warm enough for life. The problem is enhancement: burning fossil fuels, deforestation, and agriculture add extra CO₂, methane, and nitrous oxide, trapping more heat and disrupting the global climate balance.

content.question: How do vaccines help the immune system fight disease?
options: They kill all bacteria in the body
They introduce a weakened or inactive form of a pathogen so the immune system can build memory without full infection
They increase body temperature to destroy viruses
They replace white blood cells with stronger ones
content.correct_answer: They introduce a weakened or inactive form of a pathogen so the immune system can build memory without full infection
content.explanation: Vaccines expose the immune system to an antigen (part of a pathogen or a weakened form) without causing disease. The immune system creates memory B-cells. If the real pathogen appears later, the immune system recognizes and destroys it rapidly.

content.question: An atom of sodium (Na) has atomic number 11. How many electrons are in its outermost shell?
content.explanation: Sodium has 11 electrons. Filled shells: 1st shell = 2, 2nd shell = 8, leaving 11 − 10 = 1 electron in the 3rd shell. This lone valence electron explains why sodium reacts readily — it easily loses that electron to achieve a stable full shell.

content.question: A 3 kg book is lifted to a shelf 2 meters high. Using PE = mgh (g = 10 m/s²), what is its gravitational potential energy?
content.explanation: PE = mgh = 3 kg × 10 m/s² × 2 m = 60 J (joules). This energy is 'stored' — if the book falls, that potential energy converts to kinetic energy as it accelerates downward.
